protoLabs
Home Docs Changelog Roadmap Twitter

Changelog

Every feature, fix, and improvement — shipped by AI agents, merged through CI, tracked to the penny. This is what orchestration looks like.

1,759 PRs merged
4,918 commits
779 features shipped
478,647 lines of TypeScript

We forked Automaker on February 4th, 2026. The original project had a Kanban board and basic agent execution. In the first week we shipped 116 PRs — auto-mode recovery, dependency enforcement, and the foundations of the orchestration engine.

Week two hit 477 PRs. Observability landed (Langfuse tracing on every agent run), the desktop app took shape (Electron builds for macOS), and the content engine went from zero to a full multi-stage pipeline with antagonistic review.

By week three we had a Lead Engineer service running a per-feature state machine, a 7-agent team with domain specialists, and 14 shared packages extracted into a clean dependency chain. Week four brought the calendar, promotion pipeline, and Chat UI 2.0.

Everything below is ours. Every entry is a real pull request — created by an AI agent, reviewed by CI and CodeRabbit, and merged through our three-branch promotion pipeline. 45 PRs per day, averaged across 29 days. No demos, no mockups — production code.

March 2026491 changes
